topic: An athlete is asked to switch positions
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coach’s voice, usually a booming reassurance, sounded thin and sharp in the sterile locker room air. “Listen, Alex, we need you to try the center position for the next match. It’s a big change, but we need the versatility.” My palms were slick against the cold metal of the locker, a clammy sensation spreading up my forearms. I stared at my worn sneakers, the familiar pattern suddenly mocking me. Every muscle in my body seemed to seize up, a tight knot forming just beneath my ribs. When he finished speaking, I couldn't meet his eyes. I just nodded, a jerky, shallow movement, and tried to focus on the sound of my own breathing, which felt too loud, too ragged.

[story 2]

“You’re going to be fine, Maya. It’s just a shift in perspective,” the trainer had said, clapping me on the shoulder with too much force. My stomach lurched, a sickening drop in my chest that made me instinctively take a small step backward. The thought of facing the opposing team in that new role sent a wave of intense pressure through my shoulders. I kept running my tongue over my dry lips, searching for some anchor in the moment. When the coach reiterated the request, my hands clenched into fists so tight my nails bit into my palms. I swallowed hard, the movement catching in my throat like sandpaper.

[story 3]

The bright lights of the arena seemed to magnify every tremor in my hands. I watched the other athletes practicing the new formation, their movements fluid and confident, while I stood frozen near the edge of the court. A cold, heavy feeling settled in my core, making it difficult to draw a full breath. *What if I mess up? What if I fail?* The internal monologue was a frantic, buzzing static. When the coach called my name, my legs felt strangely disconnected from my body. I managed a weak, almost inaudible reply, my gaze fixed on a scuff mark on the floor, desperately trying to ground myself before I had to move.
